title: Terminal Actions
description: Configuration file for the Terminal Actions VS Code extension (.vscode/actions.json).

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference. Do not modify.

sections.description: Explicit display order of sections in the tree view. When omitted, order is derived from the actions array.

commonOnNewTerminalCommand.description: Optional command that runs once right after a new terminal is created, before action commands. This applies to all actions and is skipped when reusing an existing terminal.

id.description: Unique identifier (auto-generated by the extension). Do not modify manually.

section.description: Section/group name shown as a collapsible tree branch.

name.description: Display name of the action shown in the tree view.

command.description: Shell command to execute (e.g. "docker compose up -d ${variableName}"). Supports ${variableName} placeholders when variables are defined.

onNewTerminalCommand.description: Optional command that runs once right after a new terminal is created, before this action's command. This is skipped when reusing an existing terminal.

terminalProfile.description: Terminal profile name to use (e.g. "bash", "zsh", "PowerShell"). When omitted, the default profile is used.

reuseTerminal.description: When true (default), reuses an existing terminal for this section. When false, a new terminal is created each time.

cwd.description: Working directory for the command. Supports ${workspaceFolder} substitution.

description.description: Optional human-readable description shown as subtext in the tree view.

variables.description: Variable definitions for command placeholders. Each variable is resolved interactively before the command runs.

confirmBeforeRun.description: When true, shows a confirmation dialog before running the action.

ActionVariable.description: A variable that is resolved interactively before the command runs.

name.description: Variable name used in command placeholders (e.g. ${target}).

options.description: Predefined options shown in a quick-pick dropdown. When omitted or empty, free-text input is used.
